text:
Every Friday my friends and I would go to the park near my house. We'd grab some snacks, get some sun, maybe play a few soccer games. I liked it.

By far my favorite thing to do was hang out in the old oak tree at the corner. There was a branch just the right height for monkey bars. We'd climb those bars all afternoon, having ridiculous races around the tree. Sometimes we'd just swing around.

The thing I didn't enjoy was actually sitting. Watching the squirrels scurry between the branches always made me feel like I was missing something. I'd try to catch their nuts, imagining what they must think of us.

One particular summer, someone mentioned a bat that lived in the tree across the lawn. They said it was big and brown, hangared somewhere inside. I was fascinated.

We tried numerous times to grab one with a net, but they weren't interested. Then one day I was feeling particularly ambitious—a net would do the trick!
